title: encabarcenia rubescens
intro: Encabarcenia rubescens, also known as the desert marigold, is a flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is known for its bright yellow and orange flowers, which bloom in the spring, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The roots and leaves of Encabarcenia rubescens have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of Encabarcenia rubescens can be made into teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for a variety of uses in herbal medicine.
